titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/119

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=395 status=live modified=2021-04-06 14:55:21.052738+00 | translation moa=396 status=live modified=2021-04-06 14:55:24.158837+00

p.span:
Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis . tam presentibus quam futuris  huctredus de Grubheued . et symon filius eius et heres  Salutem . Sciatis nos
dedisse et hac carta nostra confirmasse . deo et ecclesie Sancte Marie de Melros . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus . pro animabus omnium
dominorum nostrorum . et pro animabus nostris . et patrum et matrum nostrarum . et omnium antecessorum et successorum nostrorum . et maxime comitis henrici .
illam partem terre in Elstaneshalche . que iacet ex occidentali parte ueteris aque . uersus terram eorundem monachorum quam habent de
feudo de Wittun . ad orientem terre eorum . in liberam . et puram . et perpetuam elemosinam . ut eam habeant . et teneant . et possideant . libere
et quiete . et honorifice . sicut ullam elemosinam . liberius . et quietius . et honorificentius tenent . supra omni terreno seruitio et consuetudine
. et exactione seculari . et nos et heredes nostri warantizabimus eis eandem terram contra omnes homines . et adquietabimus uersus
dominum regem . et omnes dominos nostros . de forensi seruitio . et omni terreno seruicio in perpetuum . hanc donationem fecimus
eis pro fraternitate . et orationibus . et participatione omnium bonorum eiusdem ecclesie . in sempiternum . his . T . Godardo Capellano .
Adam persona de Maccustun . Roberto . de Bernoluebi .

div.p: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, both present as well as to come, Uhtred of Grubbit, and Simon his son and heir, send greeting. Know us to have given and by this our charter to have made firm, to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, for the souls of all our lords, and for our own souls, and of our fathers' and mothers' souls, and of all our ancestors' and successors' souls, and especially of Earl Henry's soul, that pat of the land in 'Elstaneshalche', which lies on the western side of the old river, towards the land of the same monks which they have of the feu of Whitton, to the east of their land, in free, and pure, and perpetual alms, that they may have it, and hold it, and possess it, freely and quitely, and honourably, just as freely, and quitely, and honourably as they hold any alms, moreover from all earthly service and custom, and secular exaction, and we and our heirs shall warrant to them the same land against all men, and we shall discharge towards the lord king, and all our lords in respect of forinsec service, and all earthly service for ever. We have made this donation to them for the sake of fraternity, and for prayers, and for the participation in all the benefits of the same church for ever. With these witnesses: Godard the chaplain; Adam the parson of Maxton; Robert of Barnoldby.
